title: Datasheets for the derived published datasets (provenance + license)
context: Incidence-explorers publishes derived datasets (aggregated, provenanced statistics under CC-BY-4.0). Each published dataset needs a datasheet documenting its provenance, licensing, methodology, known limitations, and how to cite it correctly. This follows the 'Datasheets for Datasets' pattern and the patterns from the sibling 'cancer-dataset-datasheets' and 'open-data-datasheets' Elyos projects. Datasheets enable downstream users to correctly attribute, cite, and understand the limitations of the published data.
objective: Write datasheets for each derived published dataset produced by incidence-explorers: documenting the provenance (upstream sources, dataset versions, query parameters, extraction dates), the license (CC-BY-4.0 with upstream attribution strings preserved per the license register), methodology (how aggregate statistics were ingested and validated), known limitations (suppressed values, GLOBOCAN modelled-estimate status, registry lag, age-standardisation standards used), and how to cite the dataset correctly. Reuse patterns from the sibling 'cancer-dataset-datasheets' / 'open-data-datasheets' Elyos projects.
acceptanceCriteria: A datasheet is produced for each derived published dataset; the datasheet follows the 'Datasheets for Datasets' format and reuses patterns from the sibling cancer-dataset-datasheets / open-data-datasheets projects
Each datasheet documents provenance (upstream sources, dataset version, query parameters, extraction date, URL/DOI), license (CC-BY-4.0 with upstream attribution strings), and methodology
Each datasheet documents known limitations: suppressed values (cells based on <16 cases), GLOBOCAN estimates as modelled (not exact counts), registry lag, and age-standardisation standard(s) used
Each datasheet includes a correct citation format for the published dataset
Reviewed and signed off by the Data-governance reviewer
output: A set of datasheets for each derived published dataset — provenance, license, methodology, limitations, and citation — following the 'Datasheets for Datasets' pattern and reviewed by the data-governance reviewer.
